features.featureTitle: Add a mouth hover-triggered bite animation to the SVG smiley face in App.tsx. The current mouth is a static smile path (d="M 140 255 Q 200 315 260 255"). Replace it with an interactive mouth component that: (1) detects when the mouse enters the mouth region, (2) on mouse-enter, plays a quick 'open and bite' animation — the mouth opens wide (e.g., reveals an open ellipse or arc showing teeth/interior) for ~150–200ms, then snaps shut, (3) after biting, the mouth stays in a closed/flat or slightly-frowning state rather than the original smile, (4) when the mouse enters the mouth region again while it is closed/bitten, it repeats the open-bite-then-stay-closed sequence. The bite state resets only on the next mouse-enter. Use a React state variable (e.g., `mouthState: 'smile' | 'open' | 'bitten'`) and an SVG `<path>` or `<g>` to represent the three mouth states. The mouth's interactive hit area should use a transparent SVG `<ellipse>` or `<rect>` overlaid on the mouth region to reliably capture `onMouseEnter` events.
